seo.title: Veo 3.1 | Google AI Video Model, Specs & Pricing | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Veo 3.1 on MaxVideoAI for cinematic AI video, reference-guided control, native audio, live specs, and pricing before you render.

overview: Veo 3.1 is the current Google AI video model on MaxVideoAI for cinematic video, text-to-video, image-to-video, reference-guided workflows, native audio, and live pay-per-second pricing.

hero.title: Veo 3.1 — current Veo model for cinematic video and reference-guided control
hero.intro: Google Veo 3.1 is the current Veo 3 AI video model for cinematic video, text-to-video, image-to-video, and reference-guided workflows when you need the main Veo path for polished ads, product films, and branded sequences.

secondaryLinks.label: Compare Veo 3.1 Fast vs Veo 3.1 Lite

technicalOverview.label: Duration
technicalOverview.body: 4 s, 6 s or 8 s per render

technicalOverview.label: Resolution
technicalOverview.body: 720p or 1080p exposed in the current MaxVideoAI route at 24 fps

technicalOverview.label: Formats
technicalOverview.body: 16:9 and 9:16 across text, start-image and reference routes; 1:1 is exposed for text and first/last-frame runs

technicalOverview.label: Inputs
technicalOverview.body: Text prompts; one start image for Image-to-Video; 1–4 reference stills for Reference-to-Video; start + last frame; Extend from one existing source video

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: Native audio on by default; toggle off for silent runs

technicalOverview.label: Pricing
technicalOverview.body: Per-second; example: audio on ≈$0.40/s, audio off ≈$0.20/s

technicalOverview.label: Render timing
technicalOverview.body: Add observed render-time note (queue shows live estimates in-app)

promptStructure.title: Text-to-Video with Google Veo 3.1
promptStructure.quote: Medium shot of [subject] in [environment], [clear action] over 8 seconds. Camera [movement], 16:9 at 1080p, cinematic look with [lighting and color]. Audio: [ambience] + [music/VO cue], no subtitles.
promptStructure.description: Write prompts like a concise director’s note: cinematography, subject, action, context, style, audio, format and duration.
steps: Cinematography and framing (medium, wide, macro, tracking)
Audio cues (ambience, music, VO) and “no subtitles” if desired

tips.title: Tips & limits
items: Use framing presets and motion cues to keep Veo predictable
Add a Last frame when the opening and ending compositions must stay locked
Leverage native audio for first-pass sound; toggle off for cheap silent drafts
Keep sequences focused (2–3 beats) and reuse references for identity
Extend strong shots for longer arcs; stitch clips in your editor
Avoid tiny UI/text; overlay critical copy in post

prompts.title: Cinematic product hero (16:9, 1080p, audio on)
prompts.prompt: 8s hero shot of a premium product on a tabletop, slow dolly, branded grade, clean sonic logo on the final frame.

prompts.title: Wireless earbuds micro-story
prompts.prompt: Shot 1 (0–3 s): macro close-up of one earbud rotating on a wooden desk, shallow depth, warm lamp glow. Shot 2 (3–6 s): medium shot of a professional putting the earbuds in before stepping onto a city street, soft bokeh. Shot 3 (6–8 s): close-up of the charging case clicking shut near a laptop, soft logo reflection. Camera: smooth dollies, handheld feel. Lighting: warm interior → cool street. Audio: city ambience + soft electronic bed + VO: “Block the noise, keep the focus.” No subtitles. Negative: no brands, no on-screen text, no extreme wides.

faqs.a: On MaxVideoAI, Veo 3.1 is the current Google AI video model for short cinematic clips, text-to-video prompts, image-to-video runs, reference-guided workflows, native audio, and extension workflows.

faqs.q: Is Veo 3.1 available in Europe or the UK?
faqs.a: Yes. MaxVideoAI routes Veo jobs through supported provider endpoints, so you can render from Europe, the UK and most supported regions without separate Veo contracts.
Yes. Veo 3.1 supports 16:9 and 9:16 across the main routes; 1:1 is exposed for text and first/last-frame runs. Choose 9:16 for Reels/TikTok/Shorts and keep key action centered.
Yes. Google Veo 3.1 can start from a single still in Image-to-Video, use 1-4 reference stills in Reference-to-Video, or bridge a start and last frame.

faqs.q: How do I use Veo 3.1 for text-to-video?
faqs.a: Start with one clear subject, one action, one camera instruction, and your target format. Veo 3.1 text-to-video prompts usually work best when movement, lighting, and audio cues are explicit.
Base clips are 4/6/8 s. Use Extend from one existing source video; confirm the final duration and resolution controls in the app before generation.

faqs.q: How do I keep Veo 3.1 on-brand?
faqs.a: Use reference stills (Nano Banana or your brand library), keep character/setting descriptions consistent, and call out palette/lighting.

custom.heroDesc1: Generate short, cinematic videos with Google DeepMind's Veo 3.1 directly inside your MaxVideoAI workspace. Text-to-video, start-image animation, reference-to-video, optional last-frame guidance, extend, and native audio live in one engine.
custom.heroDesc2: Describe the scene, choose 4, 6 or 8 seconds, pick 16:9, 9:16 or 1:1, decide whether you want native audio, then generate from text, add a Start image, switch to Reference for multi-image control, or add a Last frame when you need a locked ending.
custom.heroSupportLine: Built for brand hero shots, guided start-to-end transitions, polished product reveals, and repeatable campaign variants.
heroHighlights: Directable framing||Strong composition and camera language for repeatable shots.
Sound in the same pass||Dialogue, ambience, and SFX when you want it (or keep it silent for post).
Better prompt follow-through||Clearer intent transfer from “director notes” to the final clip.
Built for continuity workflows||References + extension tools help you carry a look across multiple beats.
custom.whatTitle: What Veo 3.1 Actually Is in MaxVideoAI
custom.whatIntro1: On paper, Veo 3.1 is DeepMind’s latest short-form video model with richer audio and tighter prompt adherence.
custom.whatIntro2: In MaxVideoAI, Veo 3.1 is exposed as a controlled, production-ready engine:
custom.whatFlowTitle: MaxVideoAI wraps all of this in a simple flow:
whatFlowSteps: 2. Generate from text or add a Start image.
3. Add a Last frame only when the ending composition must stay locked.
4. Switch to Reference when you need 1–4 stills for identity or styling.
5. Set duration (4/6/8 s), aspect ratio, and resolution (720p or 1080p).
6. Compare against other engines in the same GUI.
custom.specTitle: Real Specs - Veo 3.1 route in MaxVideoAI (720p/1080p, 4-8s)
custom.specNote: These specs describe the Veo 3.1 route exposed in MaxVideoAI today. 720p/1080p are MaxVideoAI route options, not a claim about every possible Veo 3.1 provider capability.

specSections.title: Directable framing
specSections.intro: Strong at director notes, framing, and camera language for repeatable shots. Great when you need consistent brand composition.
items: Anchor composition with a hero subject.
Reuse the same shot recipe for variants.

specSections.title: Sound & continuity
specSections.intro: Sound can be generated in the same pass, and references help carry a look across beats. Keep the visual recipe stable for continuity.
items: Add light SFX or ambience cues.
Lock palette and lighting between shots.

custom.specValueProp: Veo 3.1 in MaxVideoAI gives you framing presets, native audio control, seeds and Extend—so it behaves like a directable camera, not a black box.
custom.imageTitle: Image-to-Video Workflow with Google Veo 3.1 (+ Nano Banana)
custom.imageIntro: Pair Google Veo 3.1 with Nano Banana to lock style and iterate on motion.
imageFlow: Generate 1-4 reference stills in Nano Banana (or import your brand stills).
Send them into Veo 3.1 as Reference-to-Video stills, or start from one still in Image-to-Video.
Focus your prompt on motion, timing and audio: how the camera moves, how the subject moves, and how the beat should end at 4/6/8s.
Regenerate with the same references to keep identity consistent.
imageWhy: Logo and title animations with consistent backgrounds
Short explainer visuals based on diagrams or UI stills
custom.multishotTitle: Multi-Shot & Sequenced Clips – Directed 8s Beats in Veo 3.1
custom.multishotIntro1: Veo 3.1 can compress a mini-sequence into a single 6 or 8 second clip when you write a structured prompt.
custom.multishotIntro2: Use seeds and Extend to keep framing consistent across beats.
multishotTips: One main action and one clear camera move per beat.
Keep subject, wardrobe and environment consistent; use references to lock them.
Treat it as one scene with multiple angles rather than many locations.
Extend strong shots to build 12–24s establishing sequences.
strengths: Director notes win: shot size + angle + one camera move (dolly / pan / handheld) before you describe style.
Keep one hero subject per shot; make the action physical and easy to read.
For continuity, reuse the same “shot recipe” (palette, lighting, lens feel) and change only one variable at a time.
Audio works best with minimal cues: ambience + 1 key sound, or one short VO line.
boundaries: Up to 8 seconds per render; go longer by chaining clips (or Extend).
720p and 1080p are the resolutions exposed by this MaxVideoAI route.
Tiny UI text and small lettering are unreliable — add in post.
safetyRules: No sexual content, and nothing involving minors.
No hateful, harassing, or graphic-violence content.
Don’t impersonate real people or public figures; use consent for any likeness/voice.
No non-consensual intimate imagery; don’t include private personal data (addresses, phone numbers, documents).
Keep outputs brand-safe — some prompts or reference images may be blocked by provider safety filters.
custom.comparisonTitle: Veo 3.1 vs Veo 3.1 Fast – Quick Overview
comparisonPoints: Veo 3.1 is the premium, full-fidelity tier with richer audio and motion.
Veo 3.1 Fast is cheaper and faster for drafting and social variants.
Draft in Veo 3.1 Fast, then regenerate winners in Veo 3.1 at 1080p with native audio.

relatedItems.title: Veo 3.1 Fast
relatedItems.description: Choose Fast when you need cheaper timing tests or reference-to-video drafts; stay on Veo 3.1 when the approved shot needs premium motion, audio and 1080p polish.

relatedItems.title: Kling 3 Pro
relatedItems.description: Choose Kling 3 Pro when longer controlled sequences and storyboard-style planning matter more than Veo’s short premium audio-ready finish.

relatedItems.title: Sora 2 Pro
relatedItems.description: Compare Sora 2 Pro when you are deciding between OpenAI-style concept generation and Veo 3.1’s Google route for polished brand shots.

faqs.q: Is Veo 3.1 available in Europe or the UK?
faqs.a: Yes. MaxVideoAI routes Veo jobs through supported provider endpoints, so you can render from Europe, the UK and most supported regions without separate Veo contracts.
Yes. Veo 3.1 supports 16:9 and 9:16 across the main routes; 1:1 is exposed for text and first/last-frame runs. Choose 9:16 for Reels/TikTok/Shorts and keep key action centered.
Yes. Start from a single still in Image-to-Video, use 1-4 reference stills in Reference-to-Video, or bridge a start and last frame when the ending composition must land exactly.

faqs.q: Can I extend Veo 3.1 videos beyond 8 seconds?
faqs.a: Base clips are 4/6/8 s. Use Extend from one existing source video; confirm the final duration and resolution controls in the app before generation.

faqs.q: How do I keep Veo 3.1 on-brand?
faqs.a: Use reference stills from Nano Banana or your brand library, keep character and setting descriptions consistent, and call out palette/lighting.

custom.relatedSubtitle: Compare pricing, latency, and output options across other engines available in MaxVideoAI.
custom.finalPara1: Veo 3.1 in MaxVideoAI gives you direct, pay-as-you-go access to DeepMind’s most controllable short-form video engine.
custom.finalPara2: Framing and audio controls make it feel like a virtual camera, not just another black-box generator.
custom.tipsIntro: Veo 3.1 is easiest to control when you write like a shot brief: framing, one camera move, and clear lighting cues.
troubleshootingItems: Prompt drift / ignores details → cut extra actions, move camera + framing to the first lines, and keep constraints positive (“clean background”, “centered subject”).
Motion feels messy → one move only, slower action, simpler background.
Off-brand look → lock palette + lighting, reuse the same wording across takes, use a reference frame when possible.
Text/signage breaks → keep readable text off-screen; plan to overlay critical copy in post.
VO / lip sync feels off → shorten lines and avoid long monologues.

section.title: How to Prompt Veo 3.1 by Workflow
section.intro: Veo 3.1 is not just a text model. Choose the right path in the UI: plain-language text-to-video, image-guided animation, multi-reference control, first/last bridging, or clip extension.
section.tip: Tip: Veo follows natural language well. Lead with subject, action, and context, keep one camera move, and use positive constraints. Use the specific Veo route for references, first/last, and extend.

tabs.label: Text
tabs.title: Text-to-video prompt
tabs.description: Use when the shot starts from language and Veo should infer the scene naturally.
tabs.copy:
Subject + action + context:
[Who/what does what, and where]

Camera:
[Shot size + one move]

Look:
[Lighting + palette + atmosphere]

Sound:
[Optional ambience / dialogue / SFX]

Constraint style:
Say what you want ("clean background", "steady handheld") rather than what you do not want.

tabs.label: Refs
tabs.title: Reference prompt
tabs.description: Use when the key problem is consistency, not invention.
tabs.copy:
Prompt intent:
[What the shot should accomplish]

Reference stills:
[Which images define subject / product / setting / palette]

What stays fixed:
[Wardrobe / packaging / background / mood]

What can change:
[Camera / action / framing]

Rule:
Use references to lock identity and style, then let the prompt steer the motion and camera.

tabs.label: First/Last
tabs.title: First/last frame prompt
tabs.description: Use when Veo should bridge from an opening frame to a landing frame.
tabs.copy:
First frame:
[What we open on]

Last frame:
[What we must land on]

Transition logic:
[How the camera and subject move between the two]

Continuity anchors:
[What must remain stable across the bridge]

Do not ask for unrelated scene changes in the last frame. Use it to guide the destination.

tabs.label: Extend
tabs.title: Extend prompt
tabs.description: Use when a source clip already exists and you only need the next beat.
tabs.copy:
Current clip:
[What already happened]

New beat:
[What should happen next]

What must stay continuous:
[Subject / setting / camera rhythm / lighting]

Extension rule:
Describe only the next action, reveal, or continuation. Do not rewrite the whole scene.

tabNotes.body: Refs = stabilize brand, subject, or setting.
First/Last = define the bridge between opening and landing frames.
Extend = add the next beat without breaking continuity.

globalPrinciples: Write in plain language; do not overcomplicate the brief.
Keep one clear physical action and one camera move per clip.
Tell Veo what must stay fixed when you bridge or extend a shot.
Use positive constraints and concrete atmosphere cues.
Use references to stabilize brand, wardrobe, product, or setting, not to fight the camera direction.
engineWhy: Text-to-video and single-image animation work best when the brief reads like a live-action shot.
Reference mode is best for brand, character, and product consistency.
First/last mode works when you define the opening frame, the landing frame, and one transition logic between them.
Extend works best when you describe only the new beat while preserving camera rhythm, location, and subject identity.

demo.title: Demo: wireless earbuds product micro-story
demo.prompt:
Shot 1 (0-3s): macro close-up of one wireless earbud rotating on a wooden desk, shallow depth of field, warm desk lamp glow.
Shot 2 (3-6s): medium shot of a young professional putting the earbuds on before stepping into a lively city street, soft bokeh in the background.
Shot 3 (6-8s): close-up of the charging case clicking shut beside a laptop, subtle reflections on the shell.
Camera: smooth dolly moves between beats, handheld feel but stable.
Lighting: warm interior shifting to cool evening street light, light film grain.
Audio: low city ambience, soft electronic music bed, short voiceover: "Block the noise, keep the focus." No subtitles.
Negative: no visible brands, no on-screen text, no ultra-wide distortion.
notes: The summary, prompt and demo render all describe the same wireless-earbud product ad.
The 3-beat structure fits an 8s Veo 3.1 render with native audio enabled.
